CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _content_info (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "ready" BOOLEAN, "structureVersion" VARCHAR, "version" VARCHAR, "__hash__" TEXT UNIQUE); -- structure
INSERT INTO _content_info VALUES ('checksum_content', false, 'uk2q1hXeYSt1umIm8K7SCuCyg-SnUQFYPROlGNvaTvE', 'v3.5.0--uk2q1hXeYSt1umIm8K7SCuCyg-SnUQFYPROlGNvaTvE', 'ofuQd5Gjy3_XtRyvyyW2W1o7nXpjrPbvyiOpTm9kYek'); -- meta
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _content_content; -- structure
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _content_content (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "title" VARCHAR, "body" TEXT, "description" VARCHAR, "extension" VARCHAR, "layout" VARCHAR DEFAULT 'default', "meta" TEXT, "navigation" TEXT DEFAULT true, "path" VARCHAR, "seo" TEXT DEFAULT '{}', "stem" VARCHAR, "__hash__" TEXT UNIQUE); -- structure
UPDATE _content_info SET ready = true WHERE id = 'checksum_content'; -- meta
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _content_info (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "ready" BOOLEAN, "structureVersion" VARCHAR, "version" VARCHAR, "__hash__" TEXT UNIQUE); -- structure
INSERT INTO _content_info VALUES ('checksum_blog', false, 'z3BBFYcK_rppwLBoTW3uTreqdqFc4UWhlyoT0F-NHA4', 'v3.5.0--ad3DtKXS0JNVUiGBHqCq2OJTV5sg0M7mg-A4A4Fcivc', 'Z-cTtxZ_i_7eF6tLedskZKDDAGU9rfM0MwPs5rgUdao'); -- meta
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _content_blog; -- structure
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _content_blog (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "title" VARCHAR, "author" VARCHAR NULL, "body" TEXT, "category" VARCHAR NULL, "date" VARCHAR NULL, "description" VARCHAR, "draft" BOOLEAN DEFAULT false, "extension" VARCHAR, "layout" VARCHAR DEFAULT 'blog', "meta" TEXT, "navigation" TEXT DEFAULT true, "path" VARCHAR, "seo" TEXT DEFAULT '{}', "slug" VARCHAR NULL, "stem" VARCHAR, "__hash__" TEXT UNIQUE); -- structure
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/AEE.md', 'Maintaning AEE: Another Easy Editor', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"maintaning-aee-another-easy-editor"},"Maintaning AEE: Another Easy Editor"],["p",{},["img",{"alt":"Screenshot of AEE text editor interface","src":"/uploads/aee.png"}]],["p",{},"Fuck the idea that every useful tool must be a sprawling project with a million options and a two-day onboarding. AEE exists because the Unix world forgot how to make things obvious."],["p",{},"I maintain a small fork because simplicity deserves an active steward. The original AEE did one thing extremely well: let you edit text without initiation rites. Modern toolchains and distributions broke enough build assumptions that the project stopped being reliably usable — so I fixed what needed fixing and kept the rest."],["p",{},"What I kept sacred:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"The editor''s behaviour: no modes, no key-chords, nothing that requires a manual."],["li",{},"The UI: obvious commands, visible operations, zero gatekeeping."]],["p",{},"What I changed (carefully):"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Modernized the build to compile on current toolchains (CMake + sane flags)."],["li",{},"Fixed ncurses portability and removed dead bits that only caused harm."],["li",{},"Ensured the editor runs predictably across modern Linux distros."]],["p",{},"This is preservation, not feature creep. If you want a tiny, obvious editor for quick fixes — rescue edits, config changes, emergency corrections — AEE is a refusal: a tiny resistant tool that does its single job without asking for your life story."],["p",{},"Install (AUR):"],["pre",{"className":"language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"yay -S aee-anoraktrend-git\n","language":"bash","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"yay"],["span",{"class":"stzsN"}," -S"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," aee-anoraktrend-git\n"]]]],["p",{},"Run:"],["pre",{"className":"language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"aee filename\n","language":"bash","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"aee"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," filename\n"]]]],["p",{},"Source, issues, and PRs are under ",["code",{},"anoraktrend"]," on my git hosts. INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/ee.md', 'EE: The Easy Editor', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"ee-the-easy-editor"},"EE: The Easy Editor"],["p",{},["img",{"alt":"Screenshot of EE text editor interface","src":"/uploads/ee.png"}]],["p",{},"Fuck the Unix gatekeeping that tells newcomers to \"just learn vi\" or \"RTFM\". EE was built to be obvious — a merciless rebuke to the idea that software has to be complicated to be powerful."],["p",{},"This is what good default software looks like: present the functions, label them, and get the hell out of the user''s way. EE does that. It''s installed in FreeBSD so you always have an editor that respects humans, not rituals."],["p",{},"Why EE matters:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Discoverability is not a weakness — it''s an ethical design choice."],["li",{},"EE is zero-onboarding: ",["code",{},"ee filename"]," and you edit. No memorized sequences, no panic."],["li",{},"Systems that ship useful defaults are systems designed for people, not for exclusion."]],["p",{},"Use it when you want to stop teaching people how to use your tools and start giving them tools that teach themselves."],["hr",{}],["p",{},"-# ",["em",{},"Fuck complexity. Software should work for you, not the other way around."]]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[]}}', 'TUIs', '2025-12-03', 'The Easy Editor That Actually Lives Up to Its Name', false, 'md', 'blog', '{}', 'true', '/blog/ee', '{"title":"EE: The Easy Editor","description":"The Easy Editor That Actually Lives Up to Its Name"}', 'ee', 'blog/ee', 'bz78-q4XTByHE7d1e78blgkP0YQi_CAI9SWb5YvafEs'); -- bz78-q4XTByHE7d1e78blgkP0YQi_CAI9SWb5YvafEs

This site is a practical demonstration: content written in markdown, Nuxt 4 for rendering, and Cloudflare Workers for deployment. Fast, cheap, and owned (mostly) by me."],["p",{},"Why I picked this stack: it gives me global performance without the bullshit of managed hosting and without surrendering my content to a platform that can deplatform me tomorrow."],["p",{},"Quick, no-nonsense bootstrap and dev loop:"],["pre",{"className":"language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"# principles:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Keep your content in ",["code",{},"content/"]," with frontmatter — simple, versioned, portable."],["li",{},"Use ",["code",{},"nitro.routeRules"]," to set caching for ",["code",{},"/uploads"]," and static assets."],["li",{},"Add an RSS route (",["code",{},"server/routes/rss.xml.ts"],") that reads ",["code",{},"queryContent(''/blog'')"],"."],["li",{},"Configure Nuxt to use Cloudflare Workers preset in ",["code",{},"nuxt.config.ts"],":\n",["pre",{"className":"language-typescript shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"export hosting. But compared to handing content to a hosted CMS, you keep control, avoid recurring platform lock-in, and run a site that''s fast worldwide."],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"11ty + Vercel"],": Minimal JS, fast, flexible"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Self-hosted Ghost"],": If you want a proper CMS"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Write.as"],": If you want maximum simplicity"]],["p",{},"But if you want the balance of control, performance, and modern tooling - Nuxt 4 with Cloudflare Workers is hard to beat."],["h2",{"id":"one-last-hurdle-ci"},"One Last Hurdle: CI"],["p",{},"While ",["em",{},"I"]," enjoy using my computer to build and write my website, I understand those of a less...\nactive desire to build their own software may not. I have included at least a GitHub workflow. Someone else can come along and write one for Codeberg''s Forgejo CI... maybe a future me."],["h2",{"id":"final-thoughts"},"Final Thoughts"],["p",{},"This site serves markdown content from git, deploys globally on Cloudflare''s edge, costs essentially nothing to run, and gives me complete control over every aspect."],["p",{},"No WordPress security updates. No plugin conflicts. No database backups. No server maintenance. No platform that can deplatform me."],["p",{},"Just code, content, and control."],["p",{},"If you value owning your platform, understanding your stack, and not paying monthly rent to platforms that treat you like a product - build your own site with Nuxt 3 and Cloudflare Workers."],["hr",{}],["p",{},"-# ",["em",{},"Questions about my setup? INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/mfutil.md', 'Music Folder Utils', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"music-folder-utils"},"Music Folder Utils"],["p",{},["img",{"alt":"Dolphin file manager showing music folder with album art icons","src":"/uploads/screenshot_dolphin.jpg"}]],["p",{},["strong",{},"Note:"]," I now primarily use ",["a",{"href":"https://beets.io","rel":["nofollow"]},"beets"]," for music library management. MFUtils remains available for specific desktop-integration needs, but for most library workflows I recommend evaluating Beets first."],["p",{},"Your music collection shouldn''t look like a junk drawer. If your library is full of \"Track01\" files and folders named ",["code",{},"New Folder (7)"],", you''re doing it wrong — but you don''t have to live with that mess."],["p",{},"MFUtils is my answer: a pragmatic, conservative tool that enforces good metadata, extracts cover art so your file manager actually shows album covers, and reorganizes files into a sane ",["code",{},"Artist/Album"]," layout while refusing to clobber your data."],["p",{},"Why this matters:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Ownership:"]," if you own the files, you should be able to browse them like a library, not a landfill."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Safety:"]," automated scripts that move thousands of files should be conservative and observable."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Desktop integration:"]," folder art actually matters when you use a visual file manager."]],["p",{},"What it does:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Syncs tags against MusicBrainz before moving files."],["li",{},"Extracts embedded art and writes it where GNOME/KDE will use it for folder icons."],["li",{},"Reorganizes into ",["code",{},"Artists/Artist/Album"]," with safe heuristics."],["li",{},"Creates ",["code",{},"Albums/"]," and ",["code",{},"Tracks/"]," symlink views so you can browse in multiple ways without duplication."]],["p",{},"Implementation notes:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Rewritten in Rust because I don''t trust brittle shell scripts to safely run on tens of thousands of files."],["li",{},"Parallel processing for speed, explicit error handling for safety, and validation on import to keep junk out."]],["p",{},"Use it like this:"],["pre",{"className":"language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"mfutil all ~/Music          # sync tags, reorganize, extract art, create symlinks\nmfutil sync ~/Music         # just sync metadata\nmfutil import ~/Downloads/Music ~/Music\nmfutil art ~/Music\n","language":"bash","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"mfutil"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," all"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," ~/Music"],["span",{"class":"sutJx"},"          # sync tags, reorganize, extract art, create symlinks\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"mfutil"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," sync"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," ~/Music"],["span",{"class":"sutJx"},"         # just sync metadata\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":3},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"mfutil"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," import"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," ~/Downloads/Music"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," ~/Music\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":4},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"mfutil"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," art"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," ~/Music\n"]]]],["p",{},"Install locally:"],["pre",{"className":"language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"cargo install --path .\n","language":"bash","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"cargo"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," install"],["span",{"class":"stzsN"}," --path"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," .\n"]]]],["p",{},"Project lives under ",["code",{},"anoraktrend"]," on GitHub/Codeberg. It''s GPLv3 because this is software for people who want to own their media, not hand it over to a platform."],["hr",{}],["p",{},"-# ",["em",{},"Unfortunately, this project is abandoned. Long live Beets."]],["style",{},"html pre.shiki code .sbgvK, html code.shiki .sbgvK{--shiki-light:#E2931D;--shiki-default:#6F42C1;--shiki-dark:#B392F0}html pre.shiki code .s_sjI, html code.shiki .s_sjI{--shiki-light:#91B859;--shiki-default:#032F62;--shiki-dark:#9ECBFF}html pre.shiki code .sutJx, html code.shiki INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/Quester.md', 'Quester: A Modern MPD Client for Visual Audiophiles', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"quester-a-modern-mpd-client-for-visual-audiophiles"},"Quester: A Modern MPD Client for Visual Audiophiles"],["p",{},"I''ve been obsessed with music players for as long as I can remember. From\nWinamp''s classic skinning to foobar2000''s precision, I''ve always believed that\nhow you interact with music matters almost as much as the music itself. So when\nI found myself needing a new music player for my Linux setup, I decided to\nbuild one."],["h2",{"id":"the-problem-with-existing-mpd-clients"},"The Problem with Existing MPD Clients"],["p",{},"MPD (Music Player Daemon) is a fantastic piece of software - it''s lightweight,\nreliable, and does one thing very well: play music. But most existing MPD\nclients feel... outdated. They prioritize function over form, and the visual\nexperience often takes a backseat to technical features."],["p",{},"I wanted something that:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Celebrated album art as a central part of the experience"],["li",{},"Provided visual feedback that enhanced the music"],["li",{},"Felt modern and fluid to use"],["li",{},"Still respected the core principles of MPD (lightweight, reliable, focused)"]],["h2",{"id":"enter-quester"},"Enter Quester"],["p",{},"Quester is my answer to this problem. It''s a desktop MPD client built with Qt 6\nand QML that puts visual experience at the forefront while maintaining the\nsimplicity and reliability of MPD."],["h2",{"id":"key-features"},"Key Features"],["h3",{"id":"album-focused-navigation"},"Album-Focused Navigation"],["p",{},"The main browsing experience is centered around album art. You can navigate\nthrough your music library using:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Cover Flow:"]," A familiar 3D album browsing experience"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Grid View:"]," A clean, modern grid of album covers for quick navigation"]],["p",{},"Both views prioritize high-quality album art and make it easy to find exactly\nwhat you''re looking for."],["h3",{"id":"dual-visualizers"},"Dual Visualizers"],["p",{},"Music is not just auditory - it''s visual too. Quester includes two powerful\nvisualizers:"],["h4",{"id":"spectrum-analyzer"},"Spectrum Analyzer"],["p",{},"A custom-built bar visualizer using FFTW that provides real-time frequency\nanalysis. It features:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Fully customizable color gradients"],["li",{},"Adjustable decay and sensitivity"],["li",{},"High-performance rendering for smooth visuals"]],["h4",{"id":"waveform-visualizer"},"Waveform Visualizer"],["p",{},"A real-time waveform display that shows the shape of the music being played. It\nis designed to be both aesthetically pleasing and technically accurate."],["h3",{"id":"smart-playlists-and-queue-management"},"Smart Playlists and Queue Management"],["p",{},"Managing your music queue is a breeze with Quester. It supports:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Smart Playlists:"]," Automatically generated playlists based on your\nlistening habits and music metadata"],["li",{},["strong",{},"Dynamic Queueing:"]," Easily add, remove, and reorder tracks in your queue\nwith simple drag-and-drop actions"]],["h3",{"id":"multi-output-support"},"Multi-Output Support"],["p",{},"Quester can manage multiple MPD outputs simultaneously, allowing you to control\nmusic in different rooms or on different devices from a single interface."],["h2",{"id":"technical-details"},"Technical Details"],["p",{},"Quester is built with modern technologies to ensure performance and\nreliability:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Qt 6 & QML:"]," For a modern, high-performance UI that works across multiple\nplatforms"],["li",{},["strong",{},"C++:"]," For the core logic and MPD integration, ensuring speed and\nefficiency"],["li",{},["strong",{},"FFTW:"]," For fast, accurate frequency analysis in the spectrum analyzer"],["li",{},["strong",{},"TagLib:"]," For robust music metadata and album art extraction"]],["h2",{"id":"getting-started"},"Getting Started"],["p",{},"To get started with Quester, you''ll need:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"A running MPD server"],["li",{},"A C++ compiler (GCC or Clang)"],["li",{},"Qt 6 development libraries"]],["p",{},"Detailed build and installation instructions can be found in the project''s\nREADME."],["h2",{"id":"conclusion"},"Conclusion"],["p",{},"Quester is more than just a music player; it''s a celebration of music and a\ntestament to the power of open-source software. Whether you''re a hardcore\naudiophile or just someone who loves music, I hope you''ll find Quester to be a\nvaluable addition to your digital life."],["p",{},"Join us on the quest for the perfect music experience!"]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[{"id":"the-problem-with-existing-mpd-clients","depth":2,"text":"The Problem with Existing MPD Clients"},{"id":"enter-quester","depth":2,"text":"Enter Quester"},{"id":"key-features","depth":2,"text":"Key Features","children":[{"id":"album-focused-navigation","depth":3,"text":"Album-Focused Navigation"},{"id":"dual-visualizers","depth":3,"text":"Dual Visualizers"},{"id":"smart-playlists-and-queue-management","depth":3,"text":"Smart Playlists and Queue Management"},{"id":"multi-output-support","depth":3,"text":"Multi-Output Support"}]},{"id":"technical-details","depth":2,"text":"Technical Details"},{"id":"getting-started","depth":2,"text":"Getting Started"},{"id":"conclusion","depth":2,"text":"Conclusion"}]}}', 'Music Player', '2026-02-06', 'Building a visually rich Music Player Daemon (MPD) client that prioritizes album art, visual feedback, and a fluid user experience using Qt 6 and QML.', false, 'md', 'blog', '{}', 'true', '/blog/quester', '{"title":"Quester: A Modern MPD Client for Visual Audiophiles","description":"Building a visually rich Music Player Daemon (MPD) client that prioritizes album art, visual feedback, and a fluid user experience using Qt 6 and QML."}', 'quester', 'blog/Quester', 'mWgBGXxol2TkbPALm-FBpFMfscY_vbmoe9ewhOugRw4'); -- mWgBGXxol2TkbPALm-FBpFMfscY_vbmoe9ewhOugRw4
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/Stack.md', 'My Self-Hosted Stack: Reclaiming Digital Autonomy', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"my-self-hosted-stack-reclaiming-digital-autonomy"},"My Self-Hosted Stack: Reclaiming Digital Autonomy"],["p",{},"I self-host because I''m done being a product. If you''re okay paying monthly rent so a corporation can monetize your files, keep doing that — I won''t."],["p",{},"What I run (short):"],["ul",{},["li",{},["code",{},"Nginx Proxy Manager Plus"]," for sane SSL, routing, and easy control."],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://git.helltop.net","rel":["nofollow"]},["code",{},"Gitea"]]," for code hosting I actually own."],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://nextcloud.helltop.net","rel":["nofollow"]},["code",{},"Nextcloud"]]," for files, calendars, and collaborative docs."],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://immich.helltop.net","rel":["nofollow"]},["code",{},"Immich"]]," for photo backups that don''t feed data into ad pipelines."],["li",{},["a",{"href":"https://jellyfin.helltop.net","rel":["nofollow"]},["code",{},"Jellyfin"]]," for media I actually possess — no streaming gatekeepers."],["li",{},"I organize music with ",["a",{"href":"https://beets.io","rel":["nofollow"]},"beets"]," before importing into Jellyfin; beets is far better at tagging, deduplication, and library hygiene than ad-hoc scripts."]],["p",{},"Why this matters:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Ownership: data under your control, not someone else''s business model."],["li",{},"Privacy: no profiling, no ad-driven features, no opaque policies."],["li",{},"Portability: containerized services you can move or back up anytime."]],["p",{},"The truth: self-hosting costs time, hardware, and occasional sweat. You''re the one responsible for backups and uptime. But the alternative is paying rent forever and surrendering control."],["p",{},"Start small:"],["ol",{},["li",{},"Repurpose an old desktop or cheap NAS."],["li",{},"Install Docker and a reverse proxy (NPMplus or Traefik)."],["li",{},"Deploy Nextcloud or Jellyfin as your first service."],["li",{},"Add authentication, automate backups, and monitor uptime."],["li",{},"Use Tailscale for secure remote access without exposing ports."]],["hr",{}],["p",{},"-# ",["em",{},"Remember: if buying isn''t owning, piracy isn''t stealing"]]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[]}}', 'Self-Hosting', '2026-02-24', 'How I built my own cloud infrastructure and told Big Tech to fuck off', false, 'md', 'blog', '{}', 'true', '/blog/stack', '{"title":"My Self-Hosted Stack: Reclaiming Digital Autonomy","description":"How I built my own cloud infrastructure and told Big Tech to fuck off"}', 'stack', 'blog/Stack', '6At9vJgHPMoeIFKLc0168a3Sdh_9srGElaLNkc4L4cA'); -- 6At9vJgHPMoeIFKLc0168a3Sdh_9srGElaLNkc4L4cA
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/statusflare.md', 'StatusFlare: Forging a Serverless Watchtower', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"statusflare-forging-a-serverless-watchtower"},"StatusFlare: Forging a Serverless Watchtower"],["p",{},"If you''re running your own infrastructure, you''re constantly looking for the \"Red Light.\" The moment a service goes down, you''re usually the last to know—unless you build a watchtower."],["p",{},"Today, I finished ",["strong",{},"StatusFlare"],", my custom-built, serverless status dashboard. But the road to getting it live turned into a full-scale tactical overhaul of my entire identity stack."],["h2",{"id":"the-architecture-serverless-indestructible"},"The Architecture: Serverless & Indestructible"],["p",{},"StatusFlare isn''t just another status page. It''s built to be fast, indestructible, and zero-maintenance."],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"The Core"],": Built on ",["strong",{},"Cloudflare Workers"]," (TypeScript). It runs at the edge, meaning it monitors my services from data centers all over the planet, not just from my home network."],["li",{},["strong",{},"The Brain"],": Powered by ",["strong",{},"Cloudflare D1"],", a serverless SQL database. It tracks every ping, every latency spike, and every outage snippet."],["li",{},["strong",{},"The Look"],": A custom \"Glassmorphism\" UI using the ",["strong",{},"Catppuccin Mocha/Latte"]," palette with Mauve highlights. It supports both light and dark modes natively."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Automated"],": A background cron job (",["code",{},"* * * * *"],") polls the entire arsenal every 60 seconds."]],["h2",{"id":"recent-tactical-upgrades"},"Recent Tactical Upgrades"],["p",{},"The project has evolved rapidly from a simple monitor into a full-scale management suite:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Incident Management System"],": I''ve implemented a manual incident reporting system. This allows me to create detailed incident logs, link them to specific services, and mark them as \"resolved\" once the fire is out."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Authelia OIDC Integration"],": The \"Identity War\" was won by integrating ",["strong",{},"Authelia OIDC"]," for administrative access. No more manual password juggling; the admin panel is now secured behind my primary SSO identity."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Advanced Health Checks"],": I added support for complex health checks, including:\n",["ul",{},["li",{},"Custom HTTP methods and headers."],["li",{},["strong",{},"OAuth2 Token Support"],": StatusFlare can now automatically acquire and cache Bearer tokens to monitor protected APIs."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Smart Snippets"],": It automatically parses JSON responses for better error visibility in the logs."]]],["li",{},["strong",{},"Dynamic Status Badges"],": Added a ",["code",{},"/badge/[service].svg"]," route that generates real-time SVG status indicators for embedding in READMEs or other dashboards."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Detailed Service Pages"],": Every service now has a dedicated history page showing the last 50 checks, latency trends, and active incidents."]],["h2",{"id":"deployment-the-forgejo-pipeline"},"Deployment: The Forgejo Pipeline"],["p",{},"Deployment is fully automated through a ",["strong",{},"Forgejo Actions"]," CI/CD pipeline."],["ol",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Trigger"],": Every push to the ",["code",{},"main"]," branch on ",["a",{"href":"https://git.helltop.net","rel":["nofollow"]},"git.helltop.net"]," triggers the runner."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Validation"],": The runner performs a full TypeScript type-check and linting pass."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Wrangler Deploy"],": Using the ",["code",{},"cloudflare/wrangler-action"],", the worker is automatically pushed to Cloudflare."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Database Migrations"],": D1 schema updates are handled via Wrangler''s migration system, ensuring the database stays in sync with the code."]],["p",{},"The result? A single ",["code",{},"git push"]," takes a feature from my local machine to the edge in under 60 seconds."],["h2",{"id":"conclusion"},"Conclusion"],["p",{},"Self-hosting isn''t just about running apps; it''s about understanding every layer of your stack. From the filesystem mount options to the OIDC handshake protocols, total control requires total comprehension. StatusFlare is the eyes of my network—and it''s never been sharper."],["p",{},"Check it out live: ",["a",{"href":"https://status.helltop.net","rel":["nofollow"]},"status.helltop.net"]]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[{"id":"the-architecture-serverless-indestructible","depth":2,"text":"The Architecture: Serverless & Indestructible"},{"id":"recent-tactical-upgrades","depth":2,"text":"Recent Tactical Upgrades"},{"id":"deployment-the-forgejo-pipeline","depth":2,"text":"Deployment: The Forgejo Pipeline"},{"id":"conclusion","depth":2,"text":"Conclusion"}]}}', 'Self-Hosting', '2026-03-09', 'How I built a dynamic monitoring system and survived the great LDAP/OIDC identity war of 2026.', false, 'md', 'blog', '{}', 'true', '/blog/statusflare', '{"title":"StatusFlare: Forging a Serverless Watchtower","description":"How I built a dynamic monitoring system and survived the great LDAP/OIDC identity war of 2026."}', 'statusflare', 'blog/statusflare', 'FBXlXJw9DnTLG1MZE57FfLnCr2RCEm0ZXRwS77agVoA'); -- FBXlXJw9DnTLG1MZE57FfLnCr2RCEm0ZXRwS77agVoA
INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/Tailscale.md', 'Tailscale: Your Private Network Without the Domain Name Bullshit', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"tailscale-your-private-network-without-the-domain-name-bullshit"},"Tailscale: Your Private Network Without the Domain Name Bullshit"],["p",{},"Tailscale is how I stop dealing with domain names, DNS headaches, and open ports. It builds a private WireGuard mesh between your devices so your laptop talks to your home server like they''re on the same LAN — without exposing anything to the internet."],["p",{},"Why I use it: because the alternative is paying for domains, juggling dynamic DNS, and exposing ports to a sea of bots. That''s not security; that''s begging for trouble."],["p",{},"Quick install (server):"],["pre",{"className":"language-bash shiki shiki-themes material-theme-lighter github-light github-dark","code":"curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh\nsudo tailscale up\n","language":"bash","meta":"","style":""},["code",{"__ignoreMap":""},["span",{"class":"line","line":1},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"curl"],["span",{"class":"stzsN"}," -fsSL"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," https://tailscale.com/install.sh"],["span",{"class":"smGrS"}," |"],["span",{"class":"sbgvK"}," sh\n"]],["span",{"class":"line","line":2},["span",{"class":"sbgvK"},"sudo"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," tailscale"],["span",{"class":"s_sjI"}," up\n"]]]],["p",{},"On clients: install the app or package, authenticate, and use ",["code",{},"tailscale ip -4"]," or ",["code",{},"tailscale status"]," to see device IPs. Enable MagicDNS for friendly hostnames like ",["code",{},"homeserver"],"."],["p",{},"Common uses:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Access services via ",["code",{},"http://100.x.x.x:PORT"]," or ",["code",{},"http://homeserver:PORT"]," with MagicDNS."],["li",{},"Advertise subnet routes to reach devices on your LAN (",["code",{},"--advertise-routes=192.168.1.0/24"],")."],["li",{},"Declare an exit node to route outbound traffic through a trusted home connection."],["li",{},"Share media: if you''re serving music, organize and tag it first with ",["a",{"href":"https://beets.io","rel":["nofollow"]},"beets"]," before exposing a Jellyfin or file share — clean metadata avoids a world of pain for clients."]],["p",{},"Docker patterns:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Install Tailscale on the host and bind service ports to the host''s Tailscale IP."],["li",{},"Or run Tailscale as a sidecar container using an auth key for an isolated identity."]],["p",{},"Security posture:"],["ul",{},["li",{},"Use ACLs in the admin console to limit who accesses which services."],["li",{},"If you don''t trust the coordination servers, self-host Headscale — but then you must run and secure it yourself."]],["p",{},"Practical bottom line: Tailscale removes DNS and port-forwarding friction and keeps your services private. It isn''t magic, but it''s the easiest sane way to access your homelab from anywhere."],["hr",{}],["p",{},"-# ",["em",{},"VPNs: Never leave home without one!"]],["style",{},"html pre.shiki code .stzsN{--shiki-light:#91B859;--shiki-default:#005CC5;--shiki-dark:#79B8FF}html pre.shiki code .s_sjI, html code.shiki .s_sjI{--shiki-light:#91B859;--shiki-default:#032F62;--shiki-dark:#9ECBFF}html pre.shiki code .smGrS, html code.shiki .smGrS{--shiki-light:#39ADB5;--shiki-default:#D73A49;--shiki-dark:#F97583}html .light .shiki span {color: var(--shiki-light);background: INSERT INTO _content_blog VALUES ('blog/blog/yams.md', 'YAMS', 'Lucy Ada Randall', '{"type":"minimark","value":[["h1",{"id":"yams"},"YAMS"],["p",{},["img",{"alt":"Screenshot of C code, to demonstrate coding","src":"/uploads/c.png"}]],["p",{},"YAMS is a small Docker media stack that did one thing wrong for my setup: it assumed where config belongs. That assumption breaks portability, so I forked it and added a simple option to set config paths."],["p",{},"For music tagging and library organization I now use ",["a",{"href":"https://beets.io","rel":["nofollow"]},"beets"],"; this fork only addresses configuration-path portability, not metadata management."],["p",{},"This is the kind of pragmatic fork that open source exists for: not a vendetta, not a rewrite, just a fix so software works on different storage layouts and NAS setups."],["p",{},"Principles behind the fork:"],["ul",{},["li",{},["strong",{},"Minimal change:"]," add the option, document it, keep compatibility."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Respect upstream:"]," open a clean PR and keep the fork available if the change isn''t accepted."],["li",{},["strong",{},"Make it deployable:"]," Docker compose examples included for common NAS layouts."]],["p",{},"If you''re running a Docker-based media stack on odd mount points or a NAS that doesn''t follow upstream assumptions, try the fork on GitLab. If upstream accepts it, even better — otherwise use the small variant and move on."],["hr",{}],["p",{},"-# ",["em",{},"Remember: if buying isn''t owning, piracy isn''t stealing"]]],"toc":{"title":"","searchDepth":2,"depth":2,"links":[]}}', 'Media Server', '2025-11-30', 'A Sweet Media Server Management utility.', false, 'md', 'blog', '{}', 'true', '/blog/yams', '{"title":"YAMS","description":"A Sweet Media Server Management utility."}', 'yams', 'blog/yams', 'gi2kZmD5Njz8ukXAKww8x__PrBQY-2Lf_9j0JcR_hlE'); -- gi2kZmD5Njz8ukXAKww8x__PrBQY-2Lf_9j0JcR_hlE
UPDATE _content_info SET ready = true WHERE id = 'checksum_blog'; -- meta
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _content_info (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "ready" BOOLEAN, "structureVersion" VARCHAR, "version" VARCHAR, "__hash__" TEXT UNIQUE); -- structure
INSERT INTO _content_info VALUES ('checksum_homepage', false, 'uPWLCQB3G7UyFuOA9cvvgQFk69tRUssO1k95-vnP0xc', 'v3.5.0---tyd_G5sY6okuIoRlx3BqQXedHr1lNrCD-t3zqn2JPI', 'XKT3aSDnezeuiNMfcXmMQXBWHW0SGwOQgk7gDT_HlQM'); -- meta
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _content_homepage; -- structure
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _content_homepage (id TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "extension" VARCHAR, "footer" TEXT, "hero" TEXT, "meta" TEXT, "sections" TEXT, "stem" VARCHAR, "__hash__" TEXT UNIQUE); -- structure
INSERT INTO _content_homepage VALUES ('homepage/1.index.json', 'json', '{"lastUpdated":"March 2026","statusUrl":"https://status.helltop.net/"}', '{"title":"Welcome to My Digital Rebellion","subtitle":"Fuck the corporate cloud. Build your own.","description":"I''m a self-hoster who''s done with watching Big Tech monetize every keystroke, harvest every click, and lock you into ecosystems designed to extract maximum profit from your digital life. This is my war room where I build tools, share tactics, and forge weapons for anyone ready to tear down the walled gardens and reclaim what''s rightfully theirs: complete control over your computing."}', '{}', '[{"id":"manifesto","title":"Manifesto","type":"text","content":"The internet is dying under the boot of surveillance capitalism. Five corporations own your digital existence. They''ve turned \"the cloud\" into a euphemism for \"rent-seeking middleman\" and \"user-friendly\" into code for \"we control everything.\"","list":[{"title":"Self-hosting everything","text":"Your email, your repos, your files, your life.
